manual_j.name: Manual J
manual_j.description: Designers shall complete Manual J in accordance with ACCA standards

items.rule: Thermal envelope check - all insulation and window values match building specifications
Design file window locations match architectural backgrounds
Ducts have been set to be in conditioned space
Ventilation and Infiltration match building specifications
Internal gains are within reasonable range per Manual J standards
Weather location matches project location

manual_s.name: Manual S
manual_s.description: Designers shall complete Manual S in accordance with ACCA standards

items.rule: Cooling equipment sizing is within Manual S requirements
Heating equipment sizing is within Manual S requirements
Air handling unit configuration correct (upflow, downflow, horizontal)
Temperature rise for gas furnace within manufacturer approved range
Supplemental heat strips meet Manual S requirements for heating capacity
Altitude adjustments made for high altitude locations (>2000' elevation)
Airflow and static pressure selection matches manufacturer published table data

equipment_location.name: Equipment Location and Duct Routing
equipment_location.description: All Rheia ductwork and equipment must be completely within the building thermal envelope

items.rule: AHU is located in conditioned space
All ductwork is located in conditioned space
Architectural/structural changes clearly labeled (dropped ceilings, thickened walls, inverted soffits, relocating beams)
I-Joist penetrations clearly located and sized on drawings
Structural drawings reviewed for conflicts with duct routing and beams/structural members
Construction details of architectural features will be shared with builder
Manifold located appropriately to integrate with surrounding architecture and structure

drawing_duct_system.name: Drawing the Duct System
drawing_duct_system.description: Draw the Rheia duct system per the Rheia Engineering Design Manual

items.rule: Transition duct between AHU and manifold drawn and sized correctly
All outlet types set correctly and clearly identified on drawing
Registers located to avoid conflict with lighting and integrate with architecture
All supply ducts routed from manifold to each register
Ducts in floor system reflect I-Joist layout/hole pattern or open web truss routing
Elbows added per 'When to Include Elbows' document
Risers have correct vertical length (ceiling height for high sidewall, +2' floor-to-floor, +5' attic-to-floor)
All risers have correct elbow location set
Register throw evaluated to eliminate hot/cold spots in each room
Ducts sized for 3" or 4" and clearly labeled on drawing
Return duct system drawn and sized correctly

airflow_static_pressure.name: Evaluate Airflow and Static Pressure
airflow_static_pressure.description: Follow steps in Rheia Engineering Design Manual for adequate airflow and system static pressure

items.rule: Coil pressure loss for dry and wet coils set per manufacturer specs
Filter pressure loss set per manufacturer specs or best practices
Room-by-room airflows within 20% or 25 CFM for all design modes and orientations
Total system pressure loss does not exceed equipment static by more than 0.025"
items.note: Not all modes required based on climate; not all orientations required based on job type

items.rule: All unnecessary duct runs deleted while maintaining adequate airflow/static
Duct runs omitted from rooms with <10 CFM requirement (with builder approval)

design_by_orientation.name: Design by Orientation
design_by_orientation.description: All orientations must be considered. Three options: orientation specific, single equipment with orientation specific duct, single equipment with single duct

items.rule: Manual J and Manual S completed for each of 8 orientations
Duct design for each of 8 orientations meets airflow and static requirements

single_equipment_orientation_duct.name: Single Equipment with Orientation Specific Duct Design

items.rule: Manual J and Manual S meet worst case scenario orientation
Duct design for each of 8 orientations meets airflow and static requirements

single_equipment_single_duct.name: Single Equipment with Single Duct Design

items.rule: Manual J and Manual S meet worst case scenario orientation
Single duct design checked at all 8 orientations meets requirements (static may be low at some - OK)

manifold_design.name: Manifold Design
manifold_design.description: Follow steps in Manifold Design Guideline for proper sizing and performance

items.rule: Duct take-off locations provide adequate space to transition from manifold to routing area
If manifold feeds floor system and is flush with subfloor/ceiling, space provided to avoid joist/truss conflict
Hole pattern follows square or hexagonal grid layout with proper spacing per Manifold Design Guide
Number of holes matches number of registers; number on each face is noted
Each hole is on manifold face most in line with duct routing direction
1" thickness of ductboard has been accounted for
Final manifold design includes length, width, and height dimensions
Final manifold design shows each face of the manifold
Final manifold design shows inlet from air handler
Section view provided when needed to convey AHU and manifold configuration
